hero_subtitle: SFTY connects on-chain wallet activity and provider risk data to deliver a unified source-of-funds view that supports effective compliance decisions and audit-ready case management.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule

problem_title: Transaction source of funds remains unclear
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to link wallet activity and transaction risk with accurate source-of-funds data. This gap slows risk assessments, weakens audit trails, and complicates the preparation of transaction-linked compliance reviews and reports.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects wallet activity, transaction risk signals and provider outputs into a unified context that supports clear source-of-funds review, case creation and audit-ready compliance evidence.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to source of funds review
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates wallet and transaction risk signals, routes source of funds alerts into cases, and preserves evidence to support structured compliance decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects wallet activity, risk signals and transaction source-of-funds data into audit-ready cases and decisions without replacing existing KYT providers or analytics tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled workflows, audit-ready evidence, provider orchestration and enterprise deployment for transaction-level source-of-funds review and bank due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how transaction source of funds supports compliance
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Show how source of funds links to transaction case workflows

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for transaction source of funds?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects wallet activity, transaction risk and source-of-funds data into a connected workflow that supports compliance decisions and case management for digital asset businesses and VASPs.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY connect wallet and transaction data?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates provider risk signals on wallets and transactions, linking them to customers and counterparties to create enriched alerts, linked cases and structured review workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve source-of-funds evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-grade logs of cases, decisions and supporting source-of-funds evidence to support traceability and defensible compliance review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening solutions, connecting their outputs without requiring replacement or disruption of current systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but turns their outputs into structured workflows, cases and reports to support operational compliance and supervisory readiness.
final_title: Connect transaction source-of-funds to compliance cases
final_description: Review how SFTY links on-chain activity and provider risk data into structured source-of-funds review workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
